title: Burn from hot oil while cooking
identify: Skin is red, painful, may blister after oil spill
avoid: Do not apply toothpaste or butter
emergency: Hospital if burn is deep or large

title: Steam burn from pressure cooker
identify: Skin burn due to steam exposure

title: Chemical burn from cleaning liquid
immediate_steps: Wash area with lots of water

title: Electric burn from exposed wire
avoid: Do not use water near electricity

title: Sunburn after long sun exposure
identify: Red, hot, painful skin after sun

title: Deep cut from kitchen knife
avoid: Do not wash deep wound immediately

title: Glass cut on foot
identify: Bleeding due to stepping on glass
avoid: Do not walk on injured foot
emergency: Hospital if glass is deep inside

title: Fainting due to heat
emergency: If unconscious for more than 1 minute

title: Asthma attack
avoid: Do not make person lie down

title: Fracture suspected in arm after fall
identify: Severe pain, swelling, cannot move arm
avoid: Do not try to straighten arm

title: Ankle sprain while walking
avoid: Do not put weight on ankle

title: Shoulder dislocation
identify: Shoulder looks out of place, severe pain
avoid: Do not try to fix yourself

title: Object stuck in eye
emergency: If object does not come out

title: Soap or chemical in eye
immediate_steps: Wash eye with water for 10 minutes

title: Severe head injury
avoid: Do not give food or drink

title: Finger cut deeply
avoid: Do not remove cloth if soaked

title: Vomiting repeatedly
avoid: Do not give solid food immediately

title: Food poisoning suspected
emergency: If blood in vomit or stool

title: Electric shock mild
avoid: Do not touch with wet hands

title: Low body temperature
avoid: Do not use hot water suddenly

title: Burns and scalds
identify: Skin becomes red, painful, may develop blisters
immediate_steps: Cool the burn under running water for at least 20 minutes
Remove tight items like rings or watches
Cover with a clean non-stick dressing
avoid: Do not apply ice, creams, butter, or oils
emergency: Seek medical help if burn is large, deep, or on sensitive areas

title: Severe bleeding
identify: Heavy and continuous blood loss, weakness or dizziness
immediate_steps: Apply firm pressure with a clean cloth or bandage
Raise the injured area if possible
avoid: Do not remove soaked dressings; add another layer instead
emergency: Call emergency services if bleeding does not stop quickly

title: Nosebleed
identify: Bleeding from the nose, often after injury or dryness
immediate_steps: Pinch the soft part of the nose for 10 minutes
avoid: Do not blow nose immediately after
emergency: Seek help if bleeding lasts more than 20 minutes

title: Choking in adult
identify: Difficulty breathing, inability to speak, clutching throat
immediate_steps: Give up to 5 back blows between shoulder blades
avoid: Do not give food or water

title: Stroke symptoms
identify: Face drooping, arm weakness, speech difficulty
avoid: Do not give food or drink

title: Fainting episode
identify: Sudden collapse, dizziness, temporary loss of consciousness
avoid: Do not give food or drink immediately

title: Broken bone injury
immediate_steps: Apply cold pack to reduce swelling
avoid: Do not attempt to realign the bone

title: Shortness of breath
identify: Difficulty breathing, rapid breathing, chest tightness
avoid: Do not make the person lie flat
emergency: Call emergency if breathing becomes severe

title: Poisoning case
identify: Nausea, vomiting, confusion, burns around mouth
avoid: Do not give food or drink

title: Cardiac arrest
immediate_steps: Start chest compressions at 100 to 120 per minute
avoid: Do not stop unless help arrives

title: Head injury
identify: Head pain, dizziness, confusion, possible loss of consciousness
immediate_steps: Keep the person still and calm
Apply a cold pack to reduce swelling
avoid: Do not move the person unnecessarily
Do not give food or drink
emergency: Seek immediate medical help if unconsciousness, vomiting, or confusion occurs

title: Seizure (fits)
identify: Sudden shaking, loss of control, possible unconsciousness
immediate_steps: Place something soft under the head
Turn them on their side after movements stop
avoid: Do not put anything in their mouth
emergency: Call emergency if seizure lasts more than 5 minutes or repeats

title: Heat exhaustion
immediate_steps: Move the person to a cool place
Cool the body with damp cloth
avoid: Do not give alcohol or caffeine
emergency: Seek help if symptoms worsen or do not improve within 30 minutes

title: Heatstroke
identify: High body temperature, confusion, lack of sweating, possible unconsciousness
immediate_steps: Cool the body rapidly using water or cloth
avoid: Do not give fluids if unconscious
emergency: This is a life-threatening condition requiring immediate care

title: Electric shock
identify: Burns, unconsciousness, difficulty breathing after contact with electricity
immediate_steps: Turn off power source if safe
Do not touch person directly if still connected
Check breathing and start CPR if needed
avoid: Do not touch with bare hands if current is active
Do not pour water on electrical source

title: Asthma attack
identify: Wheezing, shortness of breath, chest tightness
avoid: Do not make the person lie down
emergency: Call emergency if symptoms do not improve after inhaler use

title: Allergic reaction
avoid: Do not ignore swelling or breathing difficulty
emergency: Call emergency if severe reaction or breathing problems occur

title: Hypothermia
identify: Shivering, cold skin, confusion, slow breathing

title: Sprain or strain
avoid: Do not continue using injured area
emergency: Seek help if pain is severe or swelling increases

title: Chest pain
identify: Tightness or pain in chest, may spread to arm or jaw
immediate_steps: Help the person sit and rest

title: Cuts and grazes
identify: Broken skin, minor bleeding, pain around wound
immediate_steps: Clean the wound under running water
Apply antiseptic and cover with sterile dressing
avoid: Do not touch wound with dirty hands
Do not leave it uncovered if deep
emergency: Seek help if wound is deep, bleeding heavily, or shows signs of infection

title: Insect bite or sting
identify: Redness, swelling, itching or pain at bite site
immediate_steps: Wash the area with soap and water
Apply a cold compress to reduce swelling
emergency: Seek help if severe swelling, breathing difficulty, or allergic reaction occurs

title: Foreign object in eye
identify: Pain, redness, watering, difficulty opening eye
immediate_steps: Rinse the eye gently with clean water
Blink to try to remove object
avoid: Do not use sharp objects to remove debris
emergency: Seek help if object does not come out or vision is affected

title: Knocked out tooth
identify: Missing tooth, bleeding from mouth, pain
immediate_steps: Try to place it back in socket or store in milk
avoid: Do not let tooth dry out
emergency: Seek dental care immediately within 1 hour

title: Chemical in eye
immediate_steps: Rinse eye immediately with clean water for at least 20 minutes
emergency: Seek immediate medical help after rinsing

title: Diarrhea and vomiting
avoid: Do not give heavy or oily foods
emergency: Seek help if symptoms persist more than 2 days or signs of dehydration appear

title: Sunburn
identify: Red, painful skin after sun exposure
avoid: Do not expose skin to sun again
emergency: Seek help if severe blistering or fever occurs

title: Ear injury
immediate_steps: Cover lightly with sterile dressing if bleeding
avoid: Do not insert objects into ear
Do not attempt to clean deeply
emergency: Seek immediate medical help if severe pain or hearing loss occurs

title: Dehydration
identify: Dry mouth, dizziness, dark urine, fatigue
immediate_steps: Use oral rehydration solutions if available
avoid: Do not give alcohol or caffeine
emergency: Seek help if confusion, fainting, or severe weakness occurs

title: Panic attack
identify: Rapid heartbeat, sweating, shortness of breath, fear
avoid: Do not panic or leave the person alone
emergency: Seek help if symptoms do not improve or mimic heart attack

title: Animal bite
identify: Broken skin, bleeding, pain, risk of infection
immediate_steps: Wash the wound with running water and soap
Apply antiseptic and cover with dressing
avoid: Do not ignore even small bites
emergency: Seek medical help for deep wounds or unknown animal bites

title: Human bite
immediate_steps: Apply antiseptic and cover with sterile dressing
emergency: Seek medical help due to high infection risk

title: Concussion
avoid: Do not allow immediate return to activity
emergency: Seek help if vomiting, worsening headache, or unconsciousness occurs

title: Back pain from injury
identify: Pain in lower or upper back, stiffness, difficulty moving
avoid: Do not stay completely inactive for long
emergency: Seek help if numbness, weakness, or severe pain occurs

title: Migraine attack
identify: Severe headache, sensitivity to light, nausea
immediate_steps: Rest in a quiet, dark room
avoid: Do not expose to bright light or loud noise
emergency: Seek help if symptoms are severe or different from usual

title: Food poisoning
avoid: Do not eat heavy or contaminated food
emergency: Seek help if symptoms are severe or prolonged

title: Nose injury
emergency: Seek help if nose appears deformed or bleeding persists

title: Blunt eye injury
identify: Pain, swelling, bruising around eye, vision issues
avoid: Do not press on the eye
emergency: Seek immediate help if vision is affected

title: Low blood sugar
immediate_steps: Give fast-acting sugar such as juice or glucose tablets
emergency: Seek help if person becomes unconscious

title: High fever
identify: Body temperature above normal, sweating, chills
emergency: Seek help if fever is very high or lasts long

title: Burns (Electrical)
identify: Burn marks, possible unconsciousness, breathing difficulty
immediate_steps: Turn off power source if safe
avoid: Do not touch person while connected to electricity

title: Drowning
identify: Difficulty breathing, coughing, unconsciousness after water exposure

title: Shock (Medical)
identify: Pale skin, sweating, rapid breathing, weakness
avoid: Do not give food or drink

title: Severe Allergic Reaction (Anaphylaxis)
identify: Swelling of face/throat, breathing difficulty, rash

title: Eye Injury (Penetrating)
identify: Severe pain, visible object in eye, vision loss
immediate_steps: Cover eye lightly with sterile dressing

title: Finger Injury
emergency: Seek help if deformity or severe swelling occurs

title: Knee Injury
avoid: Do not put weight on injured knee
emergency: Seek help if unable to move or severe swelling

title: Skin Rash
emergency: Seek help if rash spreads or causes breathing issues

title: Vomiting Blood
avoid: Do not give food or drink

title: Abdominal Pain (Severe)
avoid: Do not give painkillers without advice

title: Frostbite
identify: Cold, pale or numb skin, possible blistering
immediate_steps: Warm the affected area slowly using body heat or warm water
avoid: Do not use direct heat like fire or hot water

title: Poisoning (Chemical Exposure)
identify: Burns, irritation, nausea after exposure to chemicals
immediate_steps: Rinse affected area with clean water

title: Foreign Object in Nose
identify: Difficulty breathing through one nostril, discomfort
immediate_steps: Try to remove by blowing gently
avoid: Do not insert tools into nose
emergency: Seek medical help if object cannot be removed

title: Foreign Object in Ear
identify: Pain, hearing difficulty, irritation in ear
immediate_steps: Tilt head to allow object to fall out
avoid: Do not insert objects into ear
emergency: Seek medical help if object is stuck

title: Bruises
avoid: Do not massage the bruise immediately
emergency: Seek help if bruise is severe or unexplained

title: Blisters
emergency: Seek help if signs of infection appear

title: Neck Pain (Injury)
emergency: Seek help if pain is severe or after trauma

title: Shoulder Injury
emergency: Seek help if severe pain or deformity

title: Toothache
identify: Pain in tooth or jaw, sensitivity
emergency: Seek dental care if pain is severe or swelling occurs

title: Swollen Ankles
avoid: Do not stand for long periods
emergency: Seek help if swelling is sudden or severe
source: NHS - Swollen Ankles, Feet and Legs

identify: Small deep hole after stepping on nail, bleeding, pain

identify: Sharp pain while swallowing after eating fish
avoid: Do not use fingers to remove

title: Tongue bite during seizure
avoid: Do not put fingers in mouth

identify: Bleeding from scalp, hair soaked with blood

identify: Coughing, breathing trouble after dust exposure
